action.reasoning: Observation: The current best balanced_accuracy is 0.8539 from iteration 16 (ensemble_fine.pt), an ensemble of two ConvNeXt-Tiny models (seed 42 and seed 123) with weights 0.95/0.05 and logit adjustment tau=1.70. The second ConvNeXt contributes very little, suggesting low diversity. Analysis: To push the score higher, I need a truly diverse model. ResNet50 (pretrained IMAGENET1K_V2) is architecturally different from ConvNeXt and should make complementary errors. Training it with the same proven recipe (natural sampling, standard CE, 20 epochs, layer-wise LR decay) and then ensembling it with the best ConvNeXt (best_iter9.pt) via TTA-aware logit-adjustment grid search is the most reliable path to improvement. Decision: 1) Write a robust tune_ensemble_fine.py that performs flip-TTA validation grid search over ensemble weight and tau. 2) Train ResNet50 for 20 epochs. 3) Run ensemble tuning (ConvNeXt + ResNet50). 4) Smoke-test and submit eval.
action.command: mkdir -p fake_test && cp /data/val.pt fake_test/test.pt && python train.py --arch resnet50 --epochs 20 --drop_path 0.1 --seed 42 --output_name resnet50_best.pt && python tune_ensemble_fine.py --ckpt1 checkpoints/best_iter9.pt --ckpt2 checkpoints/resnet50_best.pt --output checkpoints/ensemble_resnet.pt && python predict.py --data_path fake_test --checkpoint checkpoints/ensemble_resnet.pt --output fake_out.json && echo "Smoke test OK, preds count: $(python -c "import json; print(len(json.load(open('fake_out.json'))['predictions']))")"
